questions.question_text: Which name must appear on ALL real estate advertising in North Dakota?
distractors: The salesperson's personal name
The designated broker's home address
The North Dakota Real Estate Commission
questions.explanation: NDAC 70-02-03 requires that all advertising be conducted under the brokerage agency's licensed trade name. This ensures the public can identify the responsible brokerage behind every advertisement.

questions.question_text: A North Dakota salesperson places a newspaper ad featuring her name in large bold type and the brokerage name in small print below. Does this comply with advertising rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, the brokerage name must be equal or larger
distractors: Yes, any format is acceptable
Yes, if the broker approved the ad
No, only the broker's name may appear
questions.explanation: Under NDAC 70-02-03, all advertising must include the brokerage agency's licensed trade name at a size equal to or greater than the name of any salesperson, associate broker, or team. The salesperson's name cannot be more prominent.

questions.question_text: An associate broker designs a yard sign showing her name in 4-inch letters and the brokerage name in 2-inch letters. What must she change?
questions.correct_answer: Increase the brokerage name to at least 4 inches
distractors: Remove her name from the sign entirely
Add the Commission's phone number
Reduce her name to 1-inch letters
questions.explanation: The brokerage's licensed trade name must appear at a size equal to or greater than any individual licensee's name. The associate broker must either enlarge the brokerage name or reduce her own name to achieve compliance.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed agent creates a flyer using only her personal name and phone number. Her broker's name appears nowhere on the flyer. What is the primary violation?
questions.correct_answer: All ads must include the brokerage trade name
distractors: Flyers do not require any brokerage name
Only digital ads need the brokerage name
The violation is using a phone number
questions.explanation: Every piece of advertising in North Dakota must include the brokerage agency's licensed trade name. An ad without the brokerage name is a blind ad and violates NDAC 70-02-03.

questions.question_text: Under NDAC rules, which statement about the brokerage trade name in advertising is correct?
questions.correct_answer: It must be equal to or larger than agent names
distractors: It may be smaller if the broker approves
It is only required on yard signs
It must appear only in the footer
questions.explanation: The brokerage's licensed trade name must appear at a size and visibility equal to or greater than any salesperson, associate broker, or team name in all advertising materials.

questions.question_text: A real estate team of three agents creates a billboard ad. The team name appears in 48-point font, and the broker's trade name appears in 24-point font. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the broker's name must be equal or larger
distractors: Yes, team names may be any size
Yes, if the team has three or more members
No, team names cannot appear on billboards
questions.explanation: Team advertising in North Dakota must include the broker's trade name at equal or greater size and visibility than the team name. A 24-point broker name beneath a 48-point team name violates this requirement.

questions.question_text: In North Dakota, a real estate team is defined as how many licensees using a shared team name under the same broker?
distractors: Three or more licensees
Five or more licensees
Any single licensee using a team name
questions.explanation: A real estate team in North Dakota consists of two or more licensees operating under the same broker who use a shared team name in their marketing and advertising activities.

questions.question_text: A team wants to create business cards showing only the team name and contact information. Is this compliant with North Dakota rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, the broker's trade name must also appear
distractors: Yes, business cards are exempt from ad rules
Yes, if the team has broker approval
No, teams cannot use business cards
questions.explanation: Team advertising, including business cards, must include the broker's trade name at equal or greater size and visibility compared to the team name. Omitting the broker's trade name from any advertising material is non-compliant.

questions.question_text: Two agents from the same brokerage form a team called 'Premier Realty Group.' A consumer sees their ad and assumes they are an independent brokerage. What advertising element would prevent this confusion?
questions.correct_answer: Displaying the brokerage name at equal or larger size
distractors: Adding the word 'team' to every ad
Using a smaller font for the team name
Registering the team name with the state
questions.explanation: When the brokerage's licensed trade name appears at equal or greater size than the team name, consumers can clearly identify the responsible brokerage. This prevents confusion about which entity holds the licenses.

questions.question_text: A solo agent markets herself as 'Johnson Team' to appear larger. Under North Dakota rules, is this a valid team name?
questions.correct_answer: No, a team requires two or more licensees
distractors: Yes, any agent can use a team name
Yes, if the broker approves it
No, team names are never permitted
questions.explanation: A real estate team in North Dakota consists of two or more licensees under the same broker. A single licensee using a team name is misleading and does not meet the definition of a team.

questions.question_text: A licensee posts a property listing on social media without mentioning the brokerage name anywhere in the post. What type of violation is this?
distractors: A dual agency violation
A trust account violation
A continuing education violation
questions.explanation: Advertising without broker identification is called a blind ad and is prohibited in North Dakota. Internet and social media advertising must comply with the same rules as all other advertising, including displaying the brokerage's licensed trade name.

questions.question_text: A North Dakota agent advertises a property as having 'guaranteed annual appreciation of 10%.' Which advertising rule does this most likely violate?
questions.correct_answer: Advertising must be free from deception
distractors: The brokerage name size requirement
The team advertising disclosure rule
The blind ad prohibition
questions.explanation: Guaranteeing future appreciation is misleading because property values are not guaranteed. North Dakota requires all advertising to be free from deception and prohibits misrepresenting terms, policies, or services.

questions.question_text: Which of the following North Dakota advertising practices is permitted?
questions.correct_answer: Including agent and broker names at equal size
distractors: Omitting the broker name on internet ads
Guaranteeing a sale price in a flyer
Using a team name larger than the broker name
questions.explanation: An agent may include their own name alongside the broker's trade name as long as the broker's name is displayed at equal or greater size and visibility. Omitting the broker name, guaranteeing prices, and oversizing team names are all prohibited.

questions.question_text: A North Dakota agent's website lists services offered but uses vague language that overstates the agent's expertise. This could constitute which violation?
distractors: A blind ad violation
An appointed agency violation
A trust account commingling violation
questions.explanation: North Dakota advertising rules prohibit misrepresenting terms, policies, or services. Overstating expertise or capabilities on a website constitutes misrepresentation and violates advertising standards under NDAC 70-02-03.

questions.question_text: An agent posts a property video on a streaming platform. Do North Dakota advertising rules apply to this content?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, all media platforms follow the same rules
distractors: No, video content is exempt
Only if the video exceeds five minutes
Only if the platform charges for ads
questions.explanation: North Dakota advertising rules apply equally to all media, including internet, social media, and streaming platforms. The brokerage trade name must be displayed, and content must be free from deception regardless of format.
